Test Case Title: Splash screen displays on cold start
Pre-Conditions: Turfex app installed on the device; app has not been launched recently (force-stopped or fresh install)
Test Steps:
1. Locate the Turfex app icon on the device home screen or app drawer
2. Tap the app icon once to launch the application
3. Immediately observe the screen from the moment the app begins to open
4. Do not tap anything during this period
Expected Result:
1. A dedicated splash screen is shown as the first visible screen (no other screen is briefly flashed before it).
2. The Turfex logo/brand icon is rendered in the centre of the screen.
3. A loading indicator (spinner or progress animation) is visible while the app initialises.
4. The background colour of the splash matches the brand theme defined in AppTheme.

Pre-Conditions: Turfex app installed on the device
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app from a cold state
2. Start a stopwatch the moment the splash appears
3. Continue timing until the splash screen is replaced by the next screen
4. Record the elapsed duration
Expected Result:
1. The splash screen remains fully visible for at least 4 seconds before the app navigates away.
2. The minimum 4-second duration is enforced even if the backend initialisation finishes earlier (so the logo is never shown for less than 4s).
3. After the minimum duration has elapsed, the app navigates to the appropriate next screen based on auth state.

Test Case Title: Auto-redirect to Mobile Login when not logged in
Pre-Conditions: No user is currently signed in OR the app has been freshly installed; no persisted auth session exists
Test Steps:
1. Ensure the user is signed out (or perform a fresh install)
2. Close the app completely (force stop)
3. Reopen the app from the home screen
4. Wait for the splash screen to finish
Expected Result:
1. After the splash completes (~4 seconds), the app automatically navigates to the Mobile Login screen.
2. The Mobile Login screen shows the +91 prefix, mobile input field, and Send OTP button.
3. No authenticated screens are briefly visible before the redirect (no flash of Home or Dashboard).

Test Case Title: Auto-redirect to Home when user session active
Pre-Conditions: User previously signed in with role=user and the session has not been revoked
Test Steps:
1. Sign in successfully as a regular user
2. Close the app completely (kill it from recents)
3. Relaunch the app by tapping the icon
4. Wait for the splash to finish
Expected Result:
1. After the splash screen completes, the app navigates directly to /home.
2. The user is NOT routed through the Mobile Login screen (no login flashes are shown).
3. The Home screen loads with the user's personalised greeting and turf list.

Test Case Title: Auto-redirect to Owner Dashboard when owner session active
Pre-Conditions: A valid owner account (role=owner) is logged in and the auth session is persisted
Test Steps:
1. Sign in with an owner account and verify role is owner in Firestore
2. Close the app completely
3. Reopen the app from the home screen
4. Wait for splash to finish
Expected Result:
1. After splash completes, the owner is routed directly to /owner/dashboard.
2. No /home or Mobile Login screen is briefly visible.
3. The Owner Dashboard loads with today's bookings and earnings widgets.

Test Case Title: App does not crash on cold start
Pre-Conditions: Fresh device state with Turfex app installed
Test Steps:
1. Open Android Settings > Apps > Turfex > Storage and tap Clear Cache (do not clear data unless testing fresh install)
2. Force-stop the app
3. Relaunch Turfex from the home screen
4. Observe through at least the first 10 seconds of launch
Expected Result:
1. The app opens cleanly without displaying a system crash dialog.
2. No prolonged black screen appears (any black-screen moment lasts less than 1 second).
3. The splash screen renders and transitions into the next screen without visible errors.
4. No ANR (Application Not Responding) dialog appears during launch.

Test Case Title: TimeService syncs with server clock on init
Pre-Conditions: Device has an active, stable internet connection at the moment of app launch
Test Steps:
1. Ensure the device is connected to Wi-Fi or mobile data
2. Force-stop the app to ensure a cold start
3. Relaunch the app
4. Wait 5–10 seconds after the app opens
5. Inspect the log output or call TimeService.isSynced() to verify server time has been fetched
Expected Result:
1. Within a few seconds of launch, the TimeService successfully contacts the server clock.
2. TimeService.isSynced() returns true after the sync completes.
3. Calls to TimeService.nowCorrected() return a DateTime anchored to the server clock, not the device clock.
4. The sync completes without throwing an error in the log.

Test Case Title: TimeService falls back to device time without internet
Pre-Conditions: Device has no internet connection (airplane mode ON or Wi-Fi/data OFF)
Test Steps:
1. Enable airplane mode on the device
2. Force-stop the app
3. Relaunch Turfex
4. Wait for the splash to finish
5. Observe behaviour and inspect TimeService state
Expected Result:
1. The app launches without any crash despite the failed sync attempt.
2. TimeService.isSynced() returns false.
3. TimeService.nowCorrected() silently falls back to the device clock.
4. The app remains fully usable for offline-tolerant screens; no error dialogs block the user.

Test Case Title: Mobile Login screen UI elements displayed
Pre-Conditions: User is not signed in; the app is on the Mobile Login screen
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app on a signed-out state
2. Wait for the splash to finish
3. Observe the Mobile Login screen
4. Do not enter any data
Expected Result:
1. The screen shows a non-editable prefix consisting of the Indian flag icon and the text +91.
2. A single-line input field is visible, labelled for a 10-digit mobile number, accepting numeric input only.
3. A prominent Send OTP button is displayed below the input field.
4. Layout is not cut off; all elements are visible without scrolling on standard screen sizes.

Test Case Title: Valid 10-digit number starting with 6 is accepted
Test Steps:
1. Tap the mobile number input field
2. Type the digits 6000000000
3. Tap the Send OTP button
Expected Result:
1. No client-side validation error is shown below or inside the field.
2. The Send OTP button enters its loading state (spinner).
3. The Firebase phone auth request is fired.
4. On success, the app navigates to the OTP screen.

Test Case Title: Valid 10-digit number starting with 7 is accepted
Test Steps:
1. Clear any existing input in the mobile field
2. Type 7000000000
3. Tap Send OTP
Expected Result:
1. The number is accepted without validation error.
2. The Send OTP API call is triggered and navigates to the OTP screen on success.

Test Case Title: Valid 10-digit number starting with 8 is accepted
Test Steps:
1. Type 8000000000 in the mobile field
2. Tap Send OTP
Expected Result:
1. No validation error.
2. OTP is sent and navigation proceeds to the OTP screen.

Test Case Title: Valid 10-digit number starting with 9 is accepted
Test Steps:
1. Type 9000000000 in the mobile field
2. Tap Send OTP
Expected Result:
1. No validation error.
2. OTP is sent and the app navigates to the OTP screen.

Test Case Title: Mobile starting with 1 shows validation error
Test Steps:
1. Type 1234567890 in the mobile input
2. Tap Send OTP
Expected Result:
1. A validation error is shown inline: 'Enter a valid 10-digit Indian mobile number'.
2. The Send OTP API call is NOT fired (no Firebase request in logs).
3. The user remains on the Mobile Login screen.

Test Case Title: Mobile starting with 2 shows validation error
Test Steps:
1. Type 2234567890 in the mobile input
2. Tap Send OTP
Expected Result:
1. The same validation error is displayed.
2. No OTP request is sent.

Test Case Title: Mobile starting with 3 shows validation error
Expected Result:
1. Validation error displayed inline.
2. No OTP request fired.

Test Case Title: Mobile starting with 4 shows validation error
Expected Result:
1. Validation error displayed.
2. No OTP request fired.

Test Case Title: Mobile starting with 5 shows validation error
Expected Result:
1. Validation error displayed.
2. No OTP request fired.

Test Case Title: Mobile field accepts only numeric input
Test Steps:
1. Tap the mobile input field to focus it
2. Try to type the letters 'abc'
3. Try to paste the text 'hello' from the clipboard
Expected Result:
1. Alphabetic characters are rejected and do not appear in the input (blocked by inputFormatter).
2. The pasted non-numeric text is either rejected entirely or stripped to show only digits.
3. Only 0–9 characters can appear in the input.

Test Case Title: Mobile less than 10 digits shows validation error
Test Steps:
1. Type 98765 in the mobile field (5 digits only)
2. Tap Send OTP
Expected Result:
1. Inline validation error appears indicating the number must be 10 digits.
2. The Send OTP request is NOT fired.

Test Case Title: Empty mobile field shows validation error
Test Steps:
1. Leave the mobile field completely empty
2. Tap Send OTP without entering anything
Expected Result:
1. The inline error 'Mobile number is required' is shown.
2. The Send OTP request is not fired.

Test Case Title: Send OTP button shows loading state during API call
Pre-Conditions: Mobile Login screen is open with a valid 10-digit number entered
Test Steps:
1. Ensure a valid number is in the input
2. Tap Send OTP once
3. Immediately observe the Send OTP button
Expected Result:
1. The Send OTP button enters a disabled state showing a loading spinner.
2. The user cannot tap the button again while it is in loading state.
3. When the Firebase response arrives, the loading state ends and the app either navigates or shows an error.

Test Case Title: Cooldown timer prevents rapid re-send on login screen
Pre-Conditions: An OTP has just been sent from this screen
Test Steps:
1. Enter a valid 10-digit mobile number
2. Tap Send OTP once and let the call complete successfully
3. Return to Mobile Login via Change Number (if applicable) or attempt re-entry
4. Try to tap Send OTP again immediately
Expected Result:
1. A cooldown countdown is shown on the Send OTP button (e.g. 'Resend in 60s').
2. The Send OTP button is disabled until the countdown reaches zero.
3. Only after the 60-second cooldown can the user trigger Send OTP again for the same number.

Test Case Title: Successful OTP send navigates to OTP screen
Test Steps:
1. Enter a valid number (e.g. 9000000000)
2. Tap Send OTP
3. Wait for the response
Expected Result:
1. Firebase phone auth initiates and returns a verificationId.
2. The app navigates to the OTP verification screen.
3. The OTP screen receives both the verificationId and the mobile number via navigation arguments.

Test Case Title: Country code +91 and Indian flag displayed
Test Steps:
1. Open the Mobile Login screen
2. Focus on the left side of the mobile input area
3. Try to tap or edit the country code
Expected Result:
1. The Indian flag and the text '+91' are shown to the left of the mobile input.
2. The prefix is non-editable (tapping it does not open a country picker).
3. No other country codes are offered (app is India-only).

Test Case Title: OTP screen shows masked mobile number
Pre-Conditions: Navigated from Mobile Login after successfully requesting an OTP for 9123456789
Test Steps:
1. Enter mobile 9123456789 and tap Send OTP on Mobile Login
2. Wait for the navigation to the OTP screen
3. Observe the screen header/subtitle area
Expected Result:
1. The OTP screen shows the mobile in a masked form: +91XXXXXX6789 (first 6 digits masked, last 4 visible).
2. The displayed masked number matches the number that was entered on the previous screen.

Test Case Title: 6 circular dot boxes displayed for OTP input
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the OTP screen after requesting an OTP
2. Observe the OTP input area
Expected Result:
1. Exactly 6 circular input boxes are displayed in a horizontal row.
2. Each box is empty with a visible circular outline.
3. The first box is auto-focused for typing.

Test Steps:
1. Tap the OTP input area
2. Try typing letters (e.g. 'a', 'b')
3. Try typing symbols (e.g. '#', '@')
Expected Result:
1. Letters and symbols are rejected; the input boxes remain empty.
2. Only numeric characters (0–9) can populate the boxes.

Test Case Title: OTP auto-submits when 6th digit is entered
Test Steps:
1. Tap the OTP input
2. Type 6 digits one at a time (e.g. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6)
3. Do NOT tap the Verify button manually
Expected Result:
1. As each digit is typed, focus advances to the next box automatically.
2. The moment the 6th digit is entered, Firebase verification is triggered automatically without requiring a manual Verify tap.
3. A loading spinner briefly appears on the Verify button.

Test Case Title: Correct OTP for existing user navigates to Home
Pre-Conditions: Mobile belongs to a registered user (role=user); a valid OTP has been received for that number
Test Steps:
1. Enter the correct OTP received via SMS
2. Wait for auto-submit to trigger
Expected Result:
1. Firebase verification succeeds.
2. User is navigated to /home.
3. The Firestore user document has its lastLoginAt field updated to the current server timestamp.

Test Case Title: Correct OTP for new user navigates to Name screen
Pre-Conditions: Mobile is NOT yet registered in Firestore; OTP has been requested
Test Steps: 1. Enter the correct OTP for a brand-new mobile number
Expected Result:
1. Firebase verification succeeds.
2. The app detects the user document does not exist yet.
3. User is navigated to /name (the Name Entry screen).
4. The uid and mobile are passed via navigation arguments to the Name screen.

Test Case Title: Correct OTP for owner navigates to Owner Dashboard
Pre-Conditions: Mobile belongs to a role=owner account; OTP received
Test Steps: 1. Enter the correct OTP for an owner's mobile number
Expected Result:
1. Verification succeeds.
2. The user role is resolved to owner.
3. App navigates to /owner/dashboard (NOT /home).

Test Case Title: Wrong OTP shows error with remaining attempts
Pre-Conditions: OTP screen is open with a fresh verificationId (0 wrong attempts made)
Test Steps:
1. Enter an intentionally incorrect 6-digit OTP (e.g. 000000)
2. Allow auto-submit to trigger
Expected Result:
1. The inline error shows 'Incorrect OTP (X attempts left)' where X reflects remaining attempts.
2. The OTP input boxes are cleared so the user can re-enter.
3. The user remains on the OTP screen.

Test Case Title: First wrong attempt shows 2 attempts left
Pre-Conditions: OTP screen open; zero wrong attempts made yet
Test Steps:
1. Enter an incorrect OTP (first failed attempt)
2. Observe the error message
Expected Result:
1. Error shown: 'Incorrect OTP (2 attempts left)'.
2. Input boxes cleared; user may try again.

Test Case Title: Second wrong attempt shows 1 attempt left
Pre-Conditions: OTP screen open; one wrong attempt already made
Expected Result:
1. Error: 'Incorrect OTP (1 attempt left)'.
2. Input cleared.

Test Case Title: Third wrong attempt blocks the screen
Pre-Conditions: OTP screen open; two wrong attempts already made
Expected Result:
1. Error message shown indicating the user is now blocked from further attempts.
2. The OTP input boxes become disabled (grey/locked).
3. The Verify button is disabled.
4. A 'Change Number' link is prominently shown as the user's only way forward.

Test Case Title: Change Number link returns to Mobile Login
Pre-Conditions: OTP screen is in the blocked state after 3 wrong attempts
Expected Result:
1. The app navigates back to /mobile-login.
2. The mobile input field is empty and ready for a fresh entry.
3. The blocked state is cleared (new OTP flow can be started).

Pre-Conditions: User has just arrived on the OTP screen after requesting an OTP
Test Steps:
1. Land on the OTP screen immediately after Send OTP
2. Observe the Resend OTP label
Expected Result:
1. A 30-second countdown starts immediately, rendered as 'Resend OTP in 30s'.
2. The Resend OTP text is displayed in a disabled (non-tappable) colour.
3. Tapping it does nothing while disabled.

Pre-Conditions: OTP screen open with an active 30s countdown
Test Steps:
1. Observe the Resend label
2. Watch it for 5 consecutive seconds without interacting
Expected Result:
1. The counter decrements smoothly each second: 30, 29, 28, 27, 26, 25…
2. No visible skips or jumps; exactly one decrement per second.

Test Steps:
1. Wait 30 seconds without interacting with the OTP screen
2. Observe the Resend control
Expected Result:
1. When the countdown reaches 0, the label changes to 'Resend OTP'.
2. The text colour changes from disabled to enabled (brand accent colour).
3. The Resend control is now tappable.

Test Case Title: Tap Resend sends new OTP and restarts countdown
Test Steps:
1. Tap the Resend OTP label
2. Observe the screen
Expected Result:
1. A new OTP is requested from Firebase.
2. A snackbar appears with 'OTP resent' confirmation.
3. The 30-second countdown restarts from 30 and decrements again.
4. Input boxes are cleared.

Pre-Conditions: OTP screen is in blocked state after 3 wrong attempts; countdown has elapsed
Test Steps: 1. Tap Resend OTP after the countdown finishes
Expected Result:
1. wrongAttempts counter is reset to 0.
2. OTP input boxes are re-enabled and focusable.
3. Verify button becomes active.
4. The blocking UI is removed.

Test Case Title: Verify button shows spinner during verification
Test Steps:
1. Type 6 digits
2. Observe the Verify button area during auto-submit
Expected Result:
1. The Verify button displays a loading spinner while Firebase is processing.
2. The button is temporarily disabled to prevent double-submission.

Pre-Conditions: OTP was requested more than 5 minutes ago (likely expired)
Test Steps:
1. Wait 5+ minutes on the OTP screen
2. Enter the OTP that was originally received
3. Allow auto-submit
Expected Result:
1. Firebase returns a session-expired error.
2. An error message is shown: 'OTP expired. Please request a new one.'
3. OTP inputs are cleared so the user can Resend.

Test Case Title: Network error on verification handled gracefully
Pre-Conditions: OTP screen open with 6 digits ready to submit; device is offline
Test Steps:
1. Turn on airplane mode before auto-submit fires
2. Enter OTP to trigger verification
3. Observe the error
Expected Result:
1. Firebase call fails due to no network.
2. An inline error appears: 'Network error. Check your connection.'
3. The app does not crash; user can retry after reconnecting.

Test Case Title: Name screen shown only for new users
Pre-Conditions: OTP was successfully verified for a mobile number not previously registered
Test Steps:
1. Complete OTP verification for a brand-new mobile
2. Observe the next screen
Expected Result:
1. The app navigates to /name (Name Entry) after OTP success.
2. The screen receives uid and mobile via navigation arguments.
3. The name input field is empty and focused.

Test Case Title: Name screen not shown for existing users
Pre-Conditions: OTP verified for a mobile already linked to an existing user document
Test Steps: 1. Complete OTP for an existing user's mobile number
Expected Result:
1. The app skips the Name screen entirely.
2. It navigates directly to /home (for role=user) or /owner/dashboard (for role=owner).

Test Steps:
1. Tap the name input
2. Type 'Jo'
3. Tap Continue
Expected Result:
1. No validation error is shown.
2. The name is accepted and the user record is created in Firestore with name='Jo'.
3. The app navigates to /home.

Test Steps:
1. Leave the name field empty
2. Tap Continue
Expected Result:
1. Inline validation error appears: 'Name is required'.
2. No user document is created.
3. The user remains on the Name screen.

Test Steps:
1. Type the single letter 'A'
2. Tap Continue
Expected Result:
1. Validation error shown: 'Name must be at least 2 characters'.
2. User remains on the Name screen.

Test Steps:
1. Type a 51-character name (any letters)
2. Tap Continue
Expected Result:
1. Validation error: 'Name must be at most 50 characters'.
2. No user record created.

Test Case Title: Name must start with a letter
Test Steps:
1. Type '1Ahmed'
2. Tap Continue
3. Clear the field and type ' Ahmed' (leading space)
4. Tap Continue again
Expected Result:
1. Both attempts trigger the validation error 'Name must start with a letter'.
2. No user record is created.

Test Case Title: Name can only contain letters and spaces
Test Steps:
1. Type 'Ahmed123'
2. Tap Continue
3. Clear and try 'Ahmed@'
4. Tap Continue
Expected Result:
1. Validation error: 'Name can only contain letters and spaces'.
2. Neither name is accepted.

Test Steps:
1. Type 'Ahmed  Khan' (two consecutive spaces between words)
2. Tap Continue
Expected Result:
1. Validation error: 'Name cannot contain consecutive spaces'.
2. Name is not saved.

Test Case Title: Valid name creates Firestore user document
Pre-Conditions: Name screen is open; no Firestore user doc yet for this uid
Test Steps:
1. Type 'Ahmed Khan'
2. Tap Continue
3. Inspect the users/{uid} document in Firestore
Expected Result:
1. A new document is created at users/{uid} containing:
   - name: 'Ahmed Khan'
   - mobile: the mobile used for OTP
   - role: 'user'
   - isVerified: true
   - totalBookings: 0
   - totalHours: 0
   - createdAt: server timestamp (now)
   - lastLoginAt: server timestamp (now)

Test Case Title: After name entry user navigates to Home
Pre-Conditions: Valid name submitted on the Name screen
Test Steps: 1. Tap Continue with a valid name
Expected Result:
1. The Firestore user doc is created successfully.
2. The app navigates to /home.
3. The Home screen greeting uses the entered first name.

Test Steps:
1. Sign in as a user and land on Home/Dashboard
2. Force-stop the app via Recents > swipe
3. Relaunch Turfex from the home screen
Expected Result:
1. After the splash, the user is restored to their previous authenticated screen without having to re-enter mobile/OTP.
2. The Mobile Login screen is not shown.

Test Case Title: Logout from User Profile clears session
Pre-Conditions: User is signed in with role=user
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the user's Profile tab
2. Tap Logout
3. Confirm logout in the dialog
Expected Result:
1. Firebase auth session is cleared (currentUser == null).
2. Any cached user data is wiped.
3. The app navigates to /mobile-login.
4. Relaunching the app shows Mobile Login (not Home).

Test Case Title: Logout from Owner Profile clears session
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to Owner Profile
2. Tap Logout
3. Confirm
Expected Result:
1. Auth session cleared.
2. App navigates to /mobile-login.
3. Owner cannot access /owner routes without re-authentication.

Test Case Title: Logged-out user cannot access protected routes
Test Steps:
1. From the logged-out state, attempt to manually navigate to /home (via deep link or direct URL)
2. Observe
Expected Result:
1. The AuthProvider detects no session and redirects to /mobile-login.
2. No content from /home is shown before the redirect.

Test Case Title: Back button after logout does not return to protected screens
Pre-Conditions: User has just logged out and is on Mobile Login
Test Steps: 1. On /mobile-login, press the Android hardware back button
Expected Result:
1. The app remains on /mobile-login (or closes safely if the back stack is empty).
2. The app does NOT navigate back to Home, Profile, or any authenticated screen.

Test Steps: 1. While logged in as an owner, attempt to navigate to /home via deep link or code
Expected Result:
1. The AuthProvider / route guard detects role mismatch.
2. User is redirected to /owner/dashboard.

Test Case Title: User role blocks access to /owner routes
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to navigate to /owner/dashboard via deep link
Expected Result:
1. The route guard blocks access.
2. User is redirected to /home.

Test Case Title: Home loads active turf list from Firestore
Pre-Conditions: Signed in as role=user; at least one turf with isActive=true exists
Test Steps:
1. Sign in and navigate to /home
2. Wait for the turf cards to load
Expected Result:
1. The turf list loads fully within 3 seconds on a 4G connection.
2. Each card shows: turf name, sport(s), price per hour, area, average rating (stars + count), and opening hours.
3. Only turfs with isActive=true are visible.

Pre-Conditions: A turf exists with isActive=false in Firestore
Test Steps:
1. Sign in as a user
2. Navigate to /home
3. Look for the inactive turf by name
Expected Result:
1. Turfs with isActive=false are filtered out of the list.
2. The inactive turf does not appear anywhere in the home feed.

Pre-Conditions: User signed in with name set; device time is between 06:00 and 11:59
Test Steps:
1. Set device time to 08:30
2. Open /home
3. Observe greeting
Expected Result: 1. Greeting reads 'Good Morning, [FirstName]'.
1. Greeting reads 'Good Afternoon, [FirstName]'.
1. Greeting reads 'Good Evening, [FirstName]'.

Test Case Title: Greeting uses user's first name only
Pre-Conditions: User's full name in Firestore is 'Ahmed Khan'
Expected Result:
1. The greeting shows only 'Ahmed' (first word split from the full name).
2. The surname 'Khan' does not appear in the greeting.

Test Case Title: Sport filter All shows all active turfs
Pre-Conditions: Multiple active turfs exist across different sports
Test Steps:
1. Open /home
2. Tap the 'All' chip in the sport filter row
Expected Result:
1. The 'All' chip is highlighted as active.
2. All active turfs across every sport are shown.

Test Case Title: Sport filter Cricket shows only Cricket turfs
Pre-Conditions: At least one active cricket turf exists
Expected Result:
1. Only turfs whose sport includes Cricket are shown.

Test Case Title: Sport filter Football shows only Football turfs

Test Case Title: Sport filter Badminton shows only Badminton turfs
Pre-Conditions: At least one active badminton turf exists
Expected Result: 1. Only Badminton turfs are shown.

Test Case Title: Empty state shown when no turfs match filter

Test Steps:

Expected Result:
1. An empty-state illustration/icon is displayed.

3. No broken card placeholders or infinite loaders are visible.

Test Case Title: Location permission requested on first use
Pre-Conditions: Location permission has never been granted or denied for the app
Test Steps:
1. Perform a fresh install or revoke location permission
2. Sign in and open /home for the first time
Expected Result:
1. The OS location permission dialog appears requesting Allow / Deny.
2. The dialog is modal; Home continues to load beneath it.

Test Case Title: Distance badge in km when location enabled
Test Steps:
1. Grant location permission
2. Open /home
3. Observe each turf card
Expected Result:
1. Each turf card shows a distance badge such as '2.4 km' computed via Haversine from current location.
2. Distances are formatted to one decimal place.

Test Case Title: Location off badge when permission denied
Expected Result:
1. Each turf card shows a 'Location off' badge in place of a distance value.
2. No distances are computed or shown.

Test Case Title: Location sort orders by nearest first
Pre-Conditions: Location granted; multiple turfs with geo coordinates exist
Test Steps:
1. Grant location
2. Open /home
3. Enable the 'Sort by Nearest' option
Expected Result:
1. Turfs are reordered so the one with the smallest Haversine distance appears first.
2. Sort order is strictly ascending by distance.

Test Case Title: Tap turf card navigates to Turf Detail
Test Steps: 1. Tap anywhere on a turf card
Expected Result:
1. The app navigates to /turf/:id where :id matches the tapped turf's Firestore document ID.
2. The Turf Detail screen loads the correct turf data.

Test Case Title: Search pill navigates to Search screen
Test Steps: 1. Tap the search pill/bar at the top of Home
Expected Result:
1. The app navigates to /search.
2. The search input is auto-focused.

Pre-Conditions: User is on /home; user has at least one booking
Test Steps: 1. Tap the bell icon in the top-right of /home
Expected Result:
1. A bottom sheet slides up.
2. It shows the user's last 5 bookings with booking code, turf, date, status.
3. Tapping outside dismisses the sheet.

Pre-Conditions: User is currently on /home; admin/owner adds a new active turf

Expected Result:
1. The new turf card appears in the list within a few seconds without any pull-to-refresh.

Test Case Title: Search by turf name partial match
Pre-Conditions: Multiple turfs exist including one named 'TriStar Arena'
Test Steps:
1. Open /search
